results.title: Images of the absence = Imagenes de la ausencia = Buenos Aires, meine geschichte
results.creator_credit: German Kral, Film Munchen and Hochschule fur Fernsehen
results.brief_description: A soulful, poetic, presentation of an autobiographical documentary by Argentinian filmmaker German Kral, who sets out to discover why his family, in his mind, never really was a family. Born in Buenos Aires in 1968, the only child to Beatriz and Rodolfo, German Kral studied photography before leaving his country in 1991 to study film in Germany. One thing however had haunted Kral for many years - the sudden disappearance, when he was a young child, of his father after his parents’ separation and his mother’s absolute refusal to talk about it. It was almost as if his father did not exist leaving a feeling of emptiness and sadness in Kral and perhaps the reason for his own escape to Germany to seek another life, his own life. After seven years in Germany, Kral came to the realisation he had to make a film, that it was important for him to tell the story of his family, the love, the disintegration of love, of old age and death and perhaps in the process, to find some understanding as to why his parents separated and the reasons behind his father’s long absence. Using old photographs, home movies, archival film footage and intimate one-on-one interviews with each of his parents and his grandmother, German Kral delves deeper and deeper into his family history, probing and prompting both his mother and his father for their versions of the past, all the while taking the audience with him on his personal journey of discovery. This is a truly beautiful piece of work, chronicling not only his family’s stories, but also providing an evocative visual experience, in black and white, of the city of Buenos Aires, its people and its culture. Also features an archival interview with Jorge Luis Borges. Cast: Beatriz Rossi (mother), Rodolfo Kral (father), Emilia Monaco (grandmother). In Spanish with English subtitles.
results.creator_credit_for_label: German Kral, Film Munchen and Hochschule fur Fernsehen

results.title: Images of man
results.creator_credit: David Roberts, Don Murray and Film Australia
results.brief_description: A study of the various styles of Aboriginal rock art found in Kakadu National Park and Arnhem Land in the Northern Territory. Australian world authority on this subject, George Chaloupka, a field anthropologist with the Museum and Art Galleries Board of the Northern Territory, explains some of the unique and beautiful rock paintings to be found in this area.

results.title: Images and meaning
results.creator_credit: Alan Woodruff and Video Education Australasia
results.brief_description: The central objective of this program is to encourage students to develop their analytical skills in visual literacy. The program looks at how to analyse the non-print text of newspaper images. Using “The Age” as a case study it examines the production of photography, cartoons and graphic design, the process of choice and editorial decision making, how to critically analyse the meaning of these images and what shapes that meaning. It also informs students about the way media images are generated and deepens theit appreciation of the selective way the world is represented through newspapers. Suitable for Upper Secondary-Tertiary students.

results.title: Illustrations of radiation
results.brief_description:
Episode number 3 of Series “Transference of Heat”.
Explains how heat sources emit radiations both as heat waves or infra-red waves and as light waves; how objects absorbing heat radiations rise in temperature; how heat is produced in glass-houses and how such glass-houses are cooled; explains the effects of clouds on radiation from the sun; shows how the shape of the earth results in different concentrations of the sun’s radiation at the poles and at the equator.

results.title: Illustrations of convection
results.brief_description:
Episode number 1 of Series “Transference of Heat”.
The film is divided into three parts: the first part shows convection in water as illustrated by the working of a central heating system and a motor coar cooling system. The second part shows convection in air with ventilation in a room with windows first open and then closed, as examples. The third part of the film illustrates the difference between a sea breeze and a land breeze.

results.title: Illustrations of conduction
results.brief_description:
Episode number 2 of Series “Transference of Heat”.
The film is divided into three parts: the first illustrates conduction in different substances, good and bad conductors and the insulation of a refrigerator; the second shows the method of lagging boilers and hot and cold pipes; the third shows various examples of poor heat conductors - wool, fur, feathers and snow.

results.title: Illuminated lives
results.creator_credit: Ellen Besen, Margaret Pettigrew and ONF | NFB
results.brief_description: This vivid animation re-creates scenes of women at work in the meadows, markets and castles of yesteryear. It lifts women’s lives out of the anonymity of the Middle Ages and shows that then, as now, women of all classes performed a variety of tasks and played a dynamic role in society. The film was inspired by actual medieval illuminations, the painted pictures and ornate letters and designs used to decorate handwritten books of the period. It reflects the vibrant colour and naive style of perspective typical of such medieval art. The music, also inspired by the era, is performed on authentic period instruments.

results.title: Illegal abortion
results.creator_credit: Robin Spry, Guy Glover and ONF | NFB
results.brief_description: A dramatization that illustrates the situation of a young couple, particularly of the young woman, faced with an unwanted pregnancy and desiring an abortion forbidden by law. The film portrays her dilemma from the time the pregnancy is confirmed by her doctor to the harrowing moments in a back room where it is terminated by an illegal practitioner. Photographed by Robert Nichol. Edited by Roy Ayton Sound by Victor Merrill. Cast includes: Sabina Fircks, Frank Heron, Elaine Ives Cameron, Ken James, Bill Lynn, Walter Massey, Vladimir Rajewsky, David Schurmann

results.brief_description: The people of Papua New Guinea learn about the British electoral system in their first independent election, with all of its attendant contradictions.

results.title: Il Trovatore
results.creator_credit: Brian Large, Giuseppe Patroni Griffi, National Video Corporation and Radiotelevisione Italiana
results.brief_description: This production of Giuseppe Verdi’s “Il Trovatore” with Libretto by Salvatore Cammarano, in four acts, was performed at the magnificent outdoor theatre, the Arena di Verona in 1985. Set in the environs of Zaragoza, the capital of the Kingdom of Aragon, and in the mountains of Biscay, around 1412, the story consists of three intertwining sub-plots - war, love and revenge. Dramatic and stirring “Il Trovatore” features an excellent lead cast in singers Giorgio Zancanaro (Count Di Luna), Rosalind Plowright (Leonora), Fiorenza Cossotto (Azucena) and Franco Bonisolli (Manrico) supported by the Orchestra and Chorus of the Arena di Verona, chorus master Tullio Boni, conductor Reynald Giovaninetti. A Verdi masterpiece. Sung in Italian with English subtitles.
